findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: python.flask.security.open-redirect.open-redirect
findings.description: Data from request is passed to redirect(). This is an open redirect and could be exploited. Consider using 'url_for()' to generate links to known locations. If you must use a URL to unknown pages, consider using 'urlparse()' or similar and checking if the 'netloc' property is the same as your site's host name. See the references for more information.
cwe_ids: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')
findings.code_excerpt:
next_page = request.args.get('next')
            return redirect(url_for(next_page) if next_page else url_for('dashboard'))

tool_specific.lines:
next_page = request.args.get('next')
            return redirect(url_for(next_page) if next_page else url_for('dashboard'))

findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-settings.express-cookie-session-default-name
findings.description: Don’t use the default session cookie name Using the default session cookie name can open your app to attacks. The security issue posed is similar to X-Powered-By: a potential attacker can use it to fingerprint the server and target attacks accordingly.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-settings.express-cookie-session-no-domain
findings.description: Default session middleware settings: `domain` not set. It indicates the domain of the cookie; use it to compare against the domain of the server in which the URL is being requested. If they match, then check the path attribute next.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-settings.express-cookie-session-no-expires
findings.description: Default session middleware settings: `expires` not set. Use it to set expiration date for persistent cookies.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-settings.express-cookie-session-no-httponly
findings.description: Default session middleware settings: `httpOnly` not set. It ensures the cookie is sent only over HTTP(S), not client JavaScript, helping to protect against cross-site scripting attacks.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-settings.express-cookie-session-no-path
findings.description: Default session middleware settings: `path` not set. It indicates the path of the cookie; use it to compare against the request path. If this and domain match, then send the cookie in the request.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-cookie-settings.express-cookie-session-no-secure
findings.description: Default session middleware settings: `secure` not set. It ensures the browser only sends the cookie over HTTPS.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-session-hardcoded-secret.express-session-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: trailofbits.python.tarfile-extractall-traversal.tarfile-extractall-traversal
findings.description: Possible path traversal through `tarfile.open($PATH).extractall()` if the source tar is controlled by an attacker
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
A08:2025 - Software or Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal
findings.description: Detected possible user input going into a `path.join` or `path.resolve` function. This could possibly lead to a path traversal vulnerability,  where the attacker can access arbitrary files stored in the file system. Instead, be sure to sanitize or validate user input first.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: python.boto3.security.hardcoded-token.hardcoded-token
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: python.sqlalchemy.security.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query
findings.description: Avoiding SQL string concatenation: untrusted input concatenated with raw SQL query can result in SQL Injection. In order to execute raw query safely, prepared statement should be used. SQLAlchemy provides TextualSQL to easily used prepared statement with named parameters. For complex SQL composition, use SQL Expression Language or Schema Definition Language. In most cases, SQLAlchemy ORM will be a better option.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

propagated_value.svalue_abstract_content:
f"""
        SELECT id, username, email, role, status, created_at
        FROM user_profiles
        {where_clause}
        ORDER BY id
        LIMIT %s OFFSET %s
    """

findings.title: python.lang.security.insecure-hash-algorithms.insecure-hash-algorithm-sha1
findings.description: Detected SHA1 hash algorithm which is considered insecure. SHA1 is not collision resistant and is therefore not suitable as a cryptographic signature. Use SHA256 or SHA3 instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: python.lang.security.audit.non-literal-import.non-literal-import
findings.description: Untrusted user input in `importlib.import_module()` function allows an attacker to load arbitrary code. Avoid dynamic values in `importlib.import_module()` or use a whitelist to prevent running untrusted code.
cwe_ids: CWE-706: Use of Incorrectly-Resolved Name or Reference

findings.title: python.flask.security.xss.audit.explicit-unescape-with-markup.explicit-unescape-with-markup
findings.description: Detected explicitly unescaped content using 'Markup()'. This permits the unescaped data to include unescaped HTML which could result in cross-site scripting. Ensure this data is not externally controlled, or consider rewriting to not use 'Markup()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse.use-defused-xml-parse
findings.description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
